title: NodeSplittingHard
lean_code_with_comments:
/-- The *single-world intervention graph (SWIG)* of the CDMG `G`
w.r.t. a set `W Ріє G.V` of output nodes: the new CDMG over the
carrier `╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW` obtained by first node-splitting on `W` (yielding
`w^o = Sum.inl w` and `w^i = Sum.inr РЪеw, _РЪЕ`) and then
hard-intervening on the freshly introduced `W^i = Set.range Sum.inr`
copies. See `lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` definition
`def:G_node-splitting_intervention` (def 3.12 of the LN).

By construction this is exactly
`(G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn (Set.range Sum.inr)`;
the four `@[simp]` lemmas `nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_J`,
`nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_V`,
`mem_nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_E`,
`mem_nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_L` below characterise the four
components and are the gateway for every downstream SWIG rewrite. -/
noncomputable def nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn
    (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) (hW : W Ріє G.V) : CDMG (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW) :=
  (G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn
    (Set.range (Sum.inr : РєЉW Рєњ ╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW))

/-- Short-form alias for `nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn`, exposing
the LN's `\swig` macro / Richardson--Robins SWIG terminology as
the dot-notation `G.swig W hW`. The alias is purely cosmetic:
its job is to let downstream Lean prose (claim_3_11 here, and the
counterfactual / identification / iSCM machinery of chapters
4 -- 16) read like the lecture notes' `G_{\swig(W)}` rather than
the eight-syllable long-form name.

Declared as a `noncomputable abbrev` (not `def`) so that the four
`@[simp]` lemmas attached to `nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn` fire
on `G.swig W hW` terms by reducibility; a plain `def` would have
forced every downstream caller either to `unfold swig` first or to
maintain a parallel `swig_*` simp-lemma family. -/
noncomputable abbrev swig (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) (hW : W Ріє G.V) :
    CDMG (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW) :=
  G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW

/-- The *input* nodes of `G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW`
are `Sum.inl '' G.J Рѕф Set.range Sum.inr`: the original inputs
(carried under the canonical `inl` embedding) plus the fresh
intervention-input labels `W^i = Set.range Sum.inr`. This matches
the LN's `J_{swig(W)} = J Ріћ W^i` (def 3.12 item i.) under the
identification `╬▒ РЅЁ inl '' ╬▒` of `NodeSplittingOn.lean`. By
definition of the composition together with the `@[simp]` lemmas
`hardInterventionOn_J` and `nodeSplittingOn_J`. -/
@[simp] theorem nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_J
    (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) (hW : W Ріє G.V) :
    (G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW).J =
      Sum.inl '' G.J Рѕф Set.range (Sum.inr : РєЉW Рєњ ╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW) := by
  change ((G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn
      (Set.range Sum.inr)).J = _
  rw [hardInterventionOn_J, nodeSplittingOn_J]

/-- The *output* nodes of `G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW`
are `Sum.inl '' G.V`: the LN's `(V Рѕќ W) Ріћ W^o` collapses to all of
`Sum.inl '' G.V` under our identification `w^o = Sum.inl w` (so
`W^o Ріє Sum.inl '' G.V` since `W Ріє G.V`, and `(V Рѕќ W) Рѕф W = V` when
`W Ріє V`). Matches def 3.12 item ii. Proven by reducing to
`(Sum.inl '' G.V Рѕф Set.range Sum.inr) \ Set.range Sum.inr` via the
NS / HI projection simp lemmas, then noting `Sum.inl '' G.V` is
disjoint from `Set.range Sum.inr` by constructor mismatch. -/
@[simp] theorem nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_V
    (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) (hW : W Ріє G.V) :
    (G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW).V = Sum.inl '' G.V := by
  change ((G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn
      (Set.range Sum.inr)).V = _
  rw [hardInterventionOn_V, nodeSplittingOn_V, Set.union_diff_right]
  ext x
  constructor
  ┬и rintro РЪеh, _РЪЕ
    exact h
  ┬и intro h
    refine РЪеh, ?_РЪЕ
    obtain РЪеv, _, rflРЪЕ := h
    rintro РЪеw, hwРЪЕ
    cases hw

/-- *Directed-edge* membership in `G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn
W hW`: a pair `p` is a directed edge of the SWIG iff it is the
relabeling `(split1 W vРѓЂ, Sum.inl vРѓѓ)` of some original edge
`(vРѓЂ, vРѓѓ) Рѕѕ G.E`. Matches def 3.12 item iii.'s set-builder
`{v_1^i Рєњ v_2^o | v_1 Рєњ v_2 Рѕѕ E}` (source dispatches on `v_1 Рѕѕ W`
via `split1`; target is always the canonical `inl`). The post-NS
piece 2 (split edges `w^o Рєњ w^i`) of `nodeSplittingOn_E` is killed
by the HI because its target `Sum.inr w` lies in `W^i = Set.range
Sum.inr`; the post-NS piece 1's target `Sum.inl vРѓѓ` never does. -/
@[simp] theorem mem_nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_E
    (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) (hW : W Ріє G.V)
    {p : (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW) ├Ќ (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW)} :
    p Рѕѕ (G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW).E Рєћ
      РѕЃ vРѓЂ vРѓѓ, (vРѓЂ, vРѓѓ) Рѕѕ G.E РѕД p = (split1 W vРѓЂ, Sum.inl vРѓѓ) := by
  change p Рѕѕ ((G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn
      (Set.range Sum.inr)).E Рєћ _
  rw [mem_hardInterventionOn_E, mem_nodeSplittingOn_E]
  constructor
  ┬и rintro РЪеh, hnРЪЕ
    rcases h with РЪеvРѓЂ, vРѓѓ, hE, rflРЪЕ | РЪеw, rflРЪЕ
    ┬и exact РЪеvРѓЂ, vРѓѓ, hE, rflРЪЕ
    ┬и exact absurd РЪеw, rflРЪЕ hn
  ┬и rintro РЪеvРѓЂ, vРѓѓ, hE, rflРЪЕ
    refine РЪеOr.inl РЪеvРѓЂ, vРѓѓ, hE, rflРЪЕ, ?_РЪЕ
    rintro РЪеw, hwРЪЕ
    cases hw

/-- *Bidirected-edge* membership in
`G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW`: a pair `p` is a bidirected
edge of the SWIG iff it is the double-`inl` relabeling
`(Sum.inl vРѓЂ, Sum.inl vРѓѓ)` of some original `(vРѓЂ, vРѓѓ) Рѕѕ G.L`.
Matches def 3.12 item iv.'s `L_{swig(W)} = {v_1^o Рєћ v_2^o | v_1 Рєћ
v_2 Рѕѕ L}`. The HI on `W^i = Set.range Sum.inr` is a no-op on
`nodeSplittingOn_L` because both endpoints of every edge in the
post-NS `L` are of the form `Sum.inl _` (constructor mismatch with
`Sum.inr`). -/
@[simp] theorem mem_nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_L
    (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) (hW : W Ріє G.V)
    {p : (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW) ├Ќ (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW)} :
    p Рѕѕ (G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW).L Рєћ
      РѕЃ vРѓЂ vРѓѓ, (vРѓЂ, vРѓѓ) Рѕѕ G.L РѕД p = (Sum.inl vРѓЂ, Sum.inl vРѓѓ) := by
  change p Рѕѕ ((G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn
      (Set.range Sum.inr)).L Рєћ _
  rw [mem_hardInterventionOn_L, mem_nodeSplittingOn_L]
  constructor
  ┬и rintro РЪеh, _, _РЪЕ
    exact h
  ┬и rintro РЪеvРѓЂ, vРѓѓ, hL, rflРЪЕ
    refine РЪеРЪеvРѓЂ, vРѓѓ, hL, rflРЪЕ, ?_, ?_РЪЕ
    ┬и rintro РЪеw, hwРЪЕ; cases hw
    ┬и rintro РЪеw, hwРЪЕ; cases hw
lean_code_without_comments:
noncomputable def nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn
    (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) (hW : W Ріє G.V) : CDMG (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW) :=
  (G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn
    (Set.range (Sum.inr : РєЉW Рєњ ╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW))

noncomputable abbrev swig (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) (hW : W Ріє G.V) :
    CDMG (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW) :=
  G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW

@[simp] theorem nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_J
    (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) (hW : W Ріє G.V) :
    (G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW).J =
      Sum.inl '' G.J Рѕф Set.range (Sum.inr : РєЉW Рєњ ╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW) := by
  change ((G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn
      (Set.range Sum.inr)).J = _
  rw [hardInterventionOn_J, nodeSplittingOn_J]

@[simp] theorem nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_V
    (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) (hW : W Ріє G.V) :
    (G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW).V = Sum.inl '' G.V := by
  change ((G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn
      (Set.range Sum.inr)).V = _
  rw [hardInterventionOn_V, nodeSplittingOn_V, Set.union_diff_right]
  ext x
  constructor
  ┬и rintro РЪеh, _РЪЕ
    exact h
  ┬и intro h
    refine РЪеh, ?_РЪЕ
    obtain РЪеv, _, rflРЪЕ := h
    rintro РЪеw, hwРЪЕ
    cases hw

@[simp] theorem mem_nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_E
    (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) (hW : W Ріє G.V)
    {p : (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW) ├Ќ (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW)} :
    p Рѕѕ (G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW).E Рєћ
      РѕЃ vРѓЂ vРѓѓ, (vРѓЂ, vРѓѓ) Рѕѕ G.E РѕД p = (split1 W vРѓЂ, Sum.inl vРѓѓ) := by
  change p Рѕѕ ((G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn
      (Set.range Sum.inr)).E Рєћ _
  rw [mem_hardInterventionOn_E, mem_nodeSplittingOn_E]
  constructor
  ┬и rintro РЪеh, hnРЪЕ
    rcases h with РЪеvРѓЂ, vРѓѓ, hE, rflРЪЕ | РЪеw, rflРЪЕ
    ┬и exact РЪеvРѓЂ, vРѓѓ, hE, rflРЪЕ
    ┬и exact absurd РЪеw, rflРЪЕ hn
  ┬и rintro РЪеvРѓЂ, vРѓѓ, hE, rflРЪЕ
    refine РЪеOr.inl РЪеvРѓЂ, vРѓѓ, hE, rflРЪЕ, ?_РЪЕ
    rintro РЪеw, hwРЪЕ
    cases hw

@[simp] theorem mem_nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_L
    (G : CDMG ╬▒) (W : Set ╬▒) (hW : W Ріє G.V)
    {p : (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW) ├Ќ (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW)} :
    p Рѕѕ (G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW).L Рєћ
      РѕЃ vРѓЂ vРѓѓ, (vРѓЂ, vРѓѓ) Рѕѕ G.L РѕД p = (Sum.inl vРѓЂ, Sum.inl vРѓѓ) := by
  change p Рѕѕ ((G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn
      (Set.range Sum.inr)).L Рєћ _
  rw [mem_hardInterventionOn_L, mem_nodeSplittingOn_L]
  constructor
  ┬и rintro РЪеh, _, _РЪЕ
    exact h
  ┬и rintro РЪеvРѓЂ, vРѓѓ, hL, rflРЪЕ
    refine РЪеРЪеvРѓЂ, vРѓѓ, hL, rflРЪЕ, ?_, ?_РЪЕ
    ┬и rintro РЪеw, hwРЪЕ; cases hw
    ┬и rintro РЪеw, hwРЪЕ; cases hw
lean_explanation:
The Lean definition takes three arguments: a CDMG `G : CDMG ╬▒` over an ambient vertex type `╬▒ : Type*`, a subset `W : Set ╬▒` of vertices to split off as intervention targets, and a proof `hW : W Ріє G.V` that those vertices are output nodes (the LN's "$W \subseteq V$"). It produces a fresh `CDMG (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW)` over a strictly larger carrier. The dot-projection `G.nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn W hW` is the Lean spelling of the LN's subscript $G_{\swig(W)}$.

Rather than rebuild the four LN bullets field-by-field, the definition is the literal *composition* of the two sister operators introduced in def 3.10 and def 3.11: first node-split on $W$ (yielding the new carrier $\alpha \oplus W$, with $w^o = $ `Sum.inl w` and $w^i = $ `Sum.inr РЪеw, _РЪЕ`), then hard-intervene on the freshly-introduced 1-copies. In Lean: `(G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn (Set.range (Sum.inr : РєЉW Рєњ ╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW))`. The Mathlib idiom worth flagging is `Set.range f`, the image of all of `f`'s domain Рђћ here, the set of all `Sum.inr w` for $w \in W$, i.e. exactly the LN's $W^i$. The subtype `РєЉW` is `{x : ╬▒ // x Рѕѕ W}` (a vertex paired with a proof it lies in $W$), and `╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW` is Mathlib's disjoint-sum type whose two constructors `Sum.inl` and `Sum.inr` capture "$w^o \neq w^i$" by *type* rather than by hypothesis.

A `noncomputable abbrev swig` then exposes the LN's `\swig` macro / RichardsonРђЊRobins SWIG terminology as the dot-notation `G.swig W hW`. Because it is an `abbrev` (not a `def`), it reduces by definitional unfolding, so any `@[simp]` lemma stated for `nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn` fires equally on `G.swig W hW` terms.

The four `@[simp]` characterization theorems at the bottom of the file recover the LN's four bullets in LN-faithful set-builder form. `nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_J` shows that the inputs are `Sum.inl '' G.J Рѕф Set.range Sum.inr` Рђћ the LN's $J \sqcup W^i$ under the identification $\alpha \cong $ `Sum.inl '' ╬▒`. `nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_V` shows that the outputs collapse to `Sum.inl '' G.V` Рђћ the LN's $(V \setminus W) \sqcup W^o$ under $w^o = $ `Sum.inl w` (every $w \in W$ lies in $V$ by `hW`, so $W^o \subseteq $ `Sum.inl '' G.V`). `mem_nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_E` characterises a directed edge as `(split1 W vРѓЂ, Sum.inl vРѓѓ)` for some original $(v_1, v_2) \in G.E$ Рђћ the LN's $v_1^i \to v_2^o$ rule, where the helper `split1` realises $v \mapsto v^i$ (returning `Sum.inr РЪеv, _РЪЕ` if `v Рѕѕ W` and `Sum.inl v` otherwise). `mem_nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn_L` characterises a bidirected edge as `(Sum.inl vРѓЂ, Sum.inl vРѓѓ)` for some original $(v_1, v_2) \in G.L$ Рђћ the LN's $v_1^o \leftrightarrow v_2^o$ rule. Each is proved in three to ten lines by `change` plus `rw` through the underlying NS / HI simp lemmas, together with the constructor-mismatch fact that no element of `Sum.inl '' _` lies in `Set.range Sum.inr`.
design_choices:
**Composition `(G.nodeSplittingOn W hW).hardInterventionOn (Set.range Sum.inr)` rather than a fresh `CDMG` built field-by-field.** The four LN bullets line up *literally* with the two sister operators when one writes out the composition. Item i.) $J_{\swig(W)} = J \sqcup W^i$ falls out of `nodeSplittingOn_J` (which lifts $J$ via `Sum.inl`) post-adjoined with the intervention target `Set.range Sum.inr` $= W^i$. Item ii.) $V_{\swig(W)} = (V \setminus W) \sqcup W^o$ is what survives once the fresh copies are removed by the HI's set-difference on the outputs. Item iii.) $E_{\swig(W)} = \{v_1^i \to v_2^o\}$ is the post-NS piece-1 of the edges (source dispatched via `split1`, target via `Sum.inl`) Рђћ piece 2 (the fresh split edges $w^o \to w^i$) has target in `Set.range Sum.inr` and is killed by the HI. Item iv.) $L_{\swig(W)} = \{v_1^o \leftrightarrow v_2^o\}$ inherits the post-NS bidirected edges unchanged because both endpoints are of the form `Sum.inl _` and so cannot lie in `Set.range Sum.inr`. Defining the SWIG as this composition reuses every structural obligation already discharged by the two building blocks (`disjoint_JV`, `E_subset`, `L_subset`, `L_irrefl`, `L_symm`, `disjoint_EL`); an independent field-by-field rebuild would re-prove roughly fifty lines of structural lemmas, and Рђћ worse Рђћ every downstream proof composing the SWIG with another `nodeSplittingOn` or `hardInterventionOn` would have to bridge a parallel API.

**Intervention target is `Set.range (Sum.inr : РєЉW Рєњ ╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW)`, the post-split image of $W^i$.** In the $\alpha \oplus W$ carrier produced by `nodeSplittingOn`, the fresh intervention-input copies $W^i$ are precisely `Sum.inr`'s image of $\uparrow W$. Hard-intervening on this set is therefore the literal LN sentence at the bottom of def 3.12: *"turn all nodes of $W^i$ into input nodes, removing all edges into $W^i$"*. This asymmetry Рђћ intervening on the *fresh* copies $W^i$ (lifted through `Sum.inr`) rather than the *canonical* copies $W^o$ (lifted through `Sum.inl`) Рђћ is what gives the SWIG its "single-world" character: the original outputs are preserved as observables while only the fresh $W^i$ carries the intervention.

**Short-form alias `swig` as a `noncomputable abbrev`, not a `def`.** The eight-syllable long-form `nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn` is descriptive but unwieldy in downstream proofs; `G.swig W hW` reads like the LN's $G_{\swig(W)}$ and matches the RichardsonРђЊRobins SWIG terminology used throughout the counterfactuals literature. Declared as an `abbrev`, it reduces by definitional unfolding, so the four `@[simp]` lemmas attached to `nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn` fire on `G.swig W hW` terms by reducibility. A plain `def` would have forced every downstream caller either to `unfold swig` first or to maintain a parallel `swig_*` simp-lemma family.

**Carrier `╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW` and the `Sum.inl` / `Sum.inr` convention are inherited, not redecided.** No fresh convention is introduced at this row: `Sum.inl w = w^o` (canonical observation copy, identified with the original $w$) and `Sum.inr РЪеw, _РЪЕ = w^i` (fresh intervention-input label) are fixed by the underlying node-splitting construction, where the longer justification lives Рђћ the LN's own hints "$W = W^o$" in def 3.11 and def 3.12, plus the RichardsonРђЊRobins SWIG convention $X = X^o$. The convention is worth flagging at this row because the four `@[simp]` lemmas speak in those terms (`Sum.inl '' G.J` in the input lemma, `Sum.inl '' G.V` in the output lemma, `split1 W vРѓЂ` and `Sum.inl vРѓѓ` in the directed-edge lemma, double-`Sum.inl` in the bidirected-edge lemma); a reader who skips the node-splitting source file would otherwise be surprised by constructor names appearing inside LN-faithful set-builder forms.
